items.prompt: Compute `-7 + 12` and explain the sign of the result.
items.expected_evidence: identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Compute `-4(3)` and compare the result to `4(-3)`.
items.expected_evidence: plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Evaluate `6 - 9` on a number line and with a signed-distance interpretation.
items.expected_evidence: explain how sign and direction determine location in the four-quadrant plane
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain how sign and direction determine location in the four-quadrant plane with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why subtracting a negative can increase a quantity.
items.expected_evidence: identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point.

items.prompt: Explain how sign and magnitude work together in signed-number operations.
items.expected_evidence: plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately.

items.prompt: A student says `-3 + -5 = 2`. Diagnose the sign reasoning error. Focus on axis order reversal.
items.expected_evidence: identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point.

items.prompt: A student computes `6 - (-2)` as `4`. Explain the inverse-structure mistake. Focus on origin reference instability.
items.expected_evidence: plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately.

items.prompt: A submarine is at `-120` meters and rises `35` meters. Write and solve the signed-number expression.
items.expected_evidence: identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A team loses 4 yards on one play and gains 11 on the next. Model the net change with signed numbers.
items.expected_evidence: plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves plot ordered pairs and read plotted coordinates accurately in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, compute `-8 - 5` and justify both the sign and the magnitude.
items.expected_evidence: identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify x- and y-axis roles and use the origin as a reference point on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite check on signed number meaning to decide whether the learner’s signed-number error comes from upstream whole-number structure or from current-node reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether signed number meaning is secure enough to support coordinate plane and ordered pairs
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in signed number meaning from a current-node issue in coordinate plane and ordered pairs.

items.prompt: Use a prerequisite check on position direction and spatial language to decide whether the learner’s signed-number error comes from upstream whole-number structure or from current-node reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether position direction and spatial language is secure enough to support coordinate plane and ordered pairs
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in position direction and spatial language from a current-node issue in coordinate plane and ordered pairs.
